content: Resolved: War provides net benefits to societal progress and development.

Your objections are the predictable product of a protected life—a form of intellectual softness that can only exist in the shadow of the very security structures you seek to condemn. You speak from the luxury of peace, failing to realize that your comfort is merely the dividend of historical conflict.

Let’s be precise. Humanity does not innovate through polite consensus. We progress through the brutal necessity of survival. Look at the modern world: your communication networks, your satellite navigation, even your medical breakthroughs—these were not gifted to you by altruism. They were forged in the crucible of war, extracted from the absolute urgency of national survival, and perfected under the threat of extinction. 

You decry the mechanism of war, yet you are utterly incapable of functioning without it. You denounce the fire while warming your hands by its heat. Peace is not an era of progress; it is a period of stagnation where innovation plateaus and civilization grows soft. You are not arguing for a more moral world; you are arguing for the right to remain blissfully ignorant of the machinery that sustains your existence. Admit it: you are a beneficiary of the system you claim to despise, and your moral outrage is nothing more than a convenient delusion.
